name: Surabhi Boob
education: Master of Science (MS), Electrical Engineering - Computer Networking from North Carolina State University; Bachelor of Engineering (BE), E&TC from Pune Institute of Computer Technology
headline: Engineering | Systems and Software Design | Networking Software | Microsoft | Co-founder and Advisor @Vahati for Women in STEM Empowerment
about:
Network Software Engineering • High-Performance Ethernet (L2) Networking • Hyper-virtualized Networking • SDN • Data-plane packet processing • Cross-functional / Geographical Leadership

Networking software and backend engineer with 8+ years of hands-on experience in high-speed Ethernet, NIC driver development, Software-defined Networking, and Hyper-virtualized Data Center Networking. 

Proven ability to work across engineering, PAEs, and customer teams to diagnose performance issues, root-cause packet-level failures, and deliver mission-critical fixes for telco and OEMs. Experienced in building and validating networking test frameworks, enabling customer-facing feature readiness supporting enterprise/OEM product launches.

Advocate for women empowerment and STEM awareness, educational and knowledge-sharing, mentorship activities.

experiences.title: SDEII
experiences.jobDescription: Backend software systems design and developer - helping Amazon retail merchants, vendors and enterprise customers seamlessly onboard to inbound inventory.

experiences.title: SDE ll
experiences.jobDescription:
* Designed and implemented an Ethernet hardware testing harness supporting VPC Networking across 400K+ servers, enabling safe deployments of mission-critical packet-processing SDN layer  software.

* Built and scaled a remote NIC hardware test fleet from 5 to 80 devices, automating monitoring and access for 100+ developers and improving networking validation throughput across organizations.

* Optimized packet-path performance in Software Defined Networking layer for on-premise AWS-managed customer datacenters, supporting 100Gb+ bandwidth and low-latency connectivity.

* Devised a robust networking test framework and secured a dedicated lab used for repeatable validation, regression testing, and customer-simulated scenarios with a team of 4 engineers.

* Amazon Retail: Designed the backend system to unify the enterprise onboarding experience, enhancing onboarding using AWS compute, storage, and networking solutions for processing the shipment and planning requests

experiences.title: Network Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
* Led development of Intel 100G/200G Ethernet drivers, ensuring feature readiness and stability for enterprise and cloud workloads.
* Drove product roadmap and prioritization for high-speed NIC drivers aligned with cross-team and OEM requirements to launch functional and performance features for smartNIC
* Collaborated with VMware OS engineers, Israel-based NIC HW teams, and PAEs to deliver secure, performant drivers across hypervisor and OS layers.
* Developed ESXi drivers addressing critical security vulnerabilities and improving NIC throughput.
* Improved team velocity by 35% through consistent Agile processes, cross-functional alignment, and streamlined release workflows.
* Engineered NIC drivers (40G & 100G) for Linux, Windows, and VMware ESXi, implementing advanced offloads such as SR-IOV, RSS, VMDq, VLAN tagging/stripping, VXLAN/Geneve parsing, DCB, TCP/IP offloads.
* Delivered DPDK-driven VF driver fixes, including debugging of VLAN extraction, metadata handling, and SR-IOV bring-up interactions.
* Partnered with VMware’s virtual switch SDK team to ensure proper alignment between NIC-level offloads and hypervisor-level switching behavior.
* Resolved 30+ customer-critical issues, including a firmware-level VLAN stripping failure impacting the IBM/HP 100G NIC launch - delivered through a software-based mitigation.
* Authored technical documentation, driver API specs, and performance tuning guides used by enterprise customers integrating Intel NIC solutions.
* Worked closely with PAEs on customer escalations, reproducing real-world workloads, validating fixes, and communicating detailed RCAs.
* Market research on Marvell Networking products for customer experience with data center admins, pathfinding to fine-tune ESXi Intel team feature design

experiences.title: Software Reasearch and Development Intern - Cloud Technology
experiences.jobDescription: Assisting the Lenovo Data Center Group with research and technology projects in the areas of cloud and software defined infrastructure (SDS, SDN), Openstack, Linux, or other open source software projects or development tools.

experiences.title: Intern
experiences.jobDescription:
- Developed modules of the Wireless MP3 Player based on IoT using Microcontroller and WiFi SOC for MP3 data fetching from HTTP server. 
- Programmed the blocks for decoding and digital to analog data conversion of the raw PCM data in Linux environment. 
- Framed an algorithm for optimizing the DAC usage and playing the music over WiFi.

updates.postText:
I’m happy to share that I’m starting a new position as Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft! 

I am incredibly grateful for this outstanding opportunity to move Microsoft’s mission forward to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more! 

Looking forward to building high-performance and scalable virtual networking solutions for the cloud and empowering the infra that powers innovation across the world, collaborating with an incredible team - and on to continual learnings!

For professionals navigating the AI landscape and how it shapes their career, founders for their companies and AI learners looking to connect with like-minded people..

The AI Innovators forum by Founders Creative, organized by Vijay Ratthinam and Mala Ramakrishnan is a great opportunity to get started and accelerate your network, learnings along the way

It was an honor to share the findings about the AI-related career shifts, resources to learn and up-skill from while navigating our pathways. 

📌 Link to the detailed findings in the comments below.

At the forum, I heard Meerah Rajavel speak about her inspiring journey as a South Asian woman climbing the non-linear career path. Observing how she converted the lack of opportunities to creating opportunities as a CIO today at Palo Alto Networks -- I couldn't agree more on the importance of the 3 key things - the Right People, Quality Resources and Domain Knowledge to grow your career! I was mind-blown to learn that launching a cybersecurity attack today takes less than an hour because just as deploying developments faster, attacks are now easy to launch!!

The AI leaders panel discussion helped us understand the layers of complexity within AI implementations inside the companies from user catalogues at Instacart all the way to the finance industry culture shifts into AI adaptation, thanks to Sonali Parthasarathy Nadia Patel, CFA @Shobhina Upadhyaya, Chalisa Prarasri for sharing your take!

Shoutout to the Founder pitches by Khyati B. for Rocket , Tammy Schuring for Polymathic, Ishtiaque Mohammad for SowFin, Teja Kalakota for MedAlt and Renuka M. and Gagan Bhat on their ecosystem talks about the AI adaptation!

🚀 600 students. 200 projects. 120+ universities. 36 hours of creativity, grit, and innovation.

Serving as a judge at the HackPrinceton this weekend reminded me how AI is reshaping what’s possible for the next generation of innovators.

In just 36 hours, students from CS, Economics, Electrical, and even Finance backgrounds came together to create real-world AI applications across healthcare, sustainability, business, education, and entertainment.

Using tools like Replit, Claude, and more, they’re shortening dev cycles from months to hours and showing how fast the future of building is evolving.

One standout moment:
Suman Sah and Hari Om Sah built CareerLens after struggling to apply for jobs without clarity on industry-required skills - turning that pain point into an AI platform that helps students align their learning with real-world demands.
That’s innovation driven by empathy and grit.

🧠 Some innovative projects I had the privilege to judge
🏥 Healthcare & Wellness
• Synovia - improves surgical planning with real-time insights
• Persis AI Training - prevents workout injuries via AI posture analysis
• Protein Architecture - accelerates drug discovery through molecular insights

🌱 Sustainability
• SaveRe - tackles food waste with ecosystem-driven incentives

💼 Business & Enterprise
• Lyzn - a contract marketplace for SMBs to hedge financial risks
• Squarenetes - enables distributed LLM compute for faster AI performance

🎓 Education & Entertainment
• Mindmap - multimodal “personified professor” for deeper learning
• MindPad - AI mind-mapping for smarter studying
• Lume Chatbot - helps seniors learn tech via browser-based assistant
• DAW - interactive music creation using sign language
• Lumospace - reimagines property visualization
• Viral Wave AI - builds creator-economy workflows

Incredible meeting Suman Sah, Hari Om Sah, Truong Dang, Arish Shahab, Rajashekar V, Linda Mukundwa, Colin Boykin, Nataly Jimenez-Cruz, Arttu Myyryläinen, Morgan Farrell, @Hasan Ali, Mayank Deoras, Pathik Sharma, Andrew Cho - and so many more who brought unstoppable energy and innovation.

Huge congratulations to the participants, organizers, and winners of HackPrinceton and Princeton University for hosting their largest hackathon yet.

Big shoutout to the sponsors fueling this builder ecosystem - OpenAI Amazon Y Combinator Anthropic Capital One Hewlett Packard Enterprise Dedalus Labs Amplitude Red Bull NordVPN NordPass @ProjectsBoard  Eragon HRT @DEShaw & Co Orcava Inc. Peregrine Photon Knot Vibe Kanban 

If you are someone interested in bridging the academia and industry together:
💬 Check out the projects on Devpost (link in comments)
🧠 Comment your project, or share which one inspired you most
🤝 If you’d like to mentor these students - let’s connect!

What these students are building today is what the world will be running on tomorrow!

#HackPrinceton #AI #Innovation #Hackathon #FutureOfWork #Education #TechForImpact #Princeton #StudentInnovation

Touching the lives of 500+ South Asian Immigrant Women from all over United States in less than 45 days with a team of just 35 people and 10+ trailblazers coming forward.

That is what the powerful team at Vahati Association achieved partnering with Asians at Amazon this International Women’s Day at Amazon's Day1 Seattle Venue!

We are writing this post not with pride, but with immense gratitude.

This journey from a caterpillar to a butterfly wouldn’t have been possible without the unwavering support of our incredible partners and mentors:

Asians at Amazon Future Force SURF Incubator Reproductive Medicine Associates (RMA Network) Sangparth Inc. vPurpose Unshackled Ventures and Greencard Inc Hami Bahadori Mehfil Nights @mindbodyzone!! 🦋

And this butterfly wouldn’t have touched so many lives without the wisdom and brilliance of our amazing speakers, who added color and depth to our mission:

Preeti Suri Sunita Shastri Magdalena Sedlik Nica Chandrayee Bhattacharyya Nitya Gupta Kirti Surya Priyadarshni Natarajan Soundarya (SB) Balasubramani Tejaswini Kumar Garima Seth Suma Bhat Sangheetha Parthasarathy Kathleen Lin, MD MSCE FACOG @Nancy Parungao!!! 

Lastly, to our wonderful audience - the vibrant flowers who bloomed with us and brought the colors of their unique lives together - this is for you.

A BIG THANK YOU to everyone who made this possible. 💐

Here’s to more lives touched, more stories shared, and more dreams realized. 

Together we are unstoppable!

💻 To access recordings, learn more and get involved: https://lnkd.in/gGUYvk5S

#VahatiAssociation #ThriveWithVahati #SouthAsianImmigrantWomen #WomenEmpowerment #InternationalWomensDay #EmotionalResilience #Entrepreneurship #WomenInTech

As an immigrant you have a chance to earn huge wealth at Vahati this time.
…….. 

I am talking about wealth of experience…..and surely that will get you the wealth you thought ;)

We have curated a forum full of incredible individuals this weekend - 8th and 9th March, who have navigated the duality of identity as immigrants and built thriving pathways ground up! 

They have walked the path you are on, and they are here to share their stories, insights, and wisdom.

Who do you want to connect with?
An author and a founder sharing knowledge for immigrants? Soundarya (SB) Balasubramani
A health advisor? Magdalena Sedlik Nica
A serial entrepreneur & philanthropist? Sunita Shastri
An investor and CEO? Preeti Suri
A classical dance artist and a CEO? Chandrayee Bhattacharyya
A tech fellow at Walmart? Priyadarshni Natarajan
A researcher and educator in AI, associated with University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign and Princeton? Suma Bhat
An expert in South Asian Somatics? Sangheetha Parthasarathy
An engineering leader with 30+ years of experience? Kirti Surya 
A product and GTM executive with 20+ years of experience? Nitya Gupta
An award winning reproductive endocrinologist? Dr Kathleen Lin
A tech leader, speaker, mentor, and storyteller? Tejaswini Kumar 
A relationship coach for immigrants? Garima Seth
A healing touch practitioner? Nancy Parungao

These inspiring individuals have taken “the road not taken” (shoutout to Robert Frost!) and carved out unique paths for themselves. 

If you have ever felt unsure about your professional identity as an immigrant then this is the event for you.
We are here to help you burst the overthinking cloud of “Who am I as a professional here?” and spread wings of clarity, connection, and growth.

So, don’t forget to sign up if you haven’t already. Here’s the link for you: https://lnkd.in/gkXNvqua 

Sign up. Engage. Interact. Grow. ✨

Let’s build your wealth of experience and everything that comes with it together!

publications.title: Distributed and Privacy Preserving Routing of Connected Vehicles to Minimize Congestion
publications.source: 2020 IEEE 17th International Conference on Mobile Ad Hoc and Sensor Systems (MASS)
publications.summary: With a large number of connected vehicles on the roads, there is an opportunity to leverage their connectivity to minimize congestion on roads by calculating fast routes for vehicles in a way that each vehicle contributes as little to the congestion as possible. The existing commercial and research based approaches of calculating routes for vehicles suffer from one or more of the following two limitations: 1) they are not privacy preserving in the sense that they receive destination addresses from users and may either store and use them for other commercial purposes or are at a risk of getting hacked and exposing these addresses to hackers; and 2) they require expensive infrastructure such as road side units (RSUs). To address these limitations, we propose a distributed and privacy preserving routing protocol, namely DPR, which the connected vehicles collaboratively and repeatedly execute to calculate fast routes to their destinations such that the overall congestion on the road network is significantly reduced and at the same time the privacy of the vehicles is preserved. The DPR protocol relies on direct vehicle to vehicle communication and does not need any new infrastructure such as RSUs. We have implemented and evaluated our DPR protocol through simulations on a real road network under several traffic conditions. Our results show that DPR reduces the average travel time of vehicles that travel a distance of 1000, 2500, and over 4000 meters by 15%, 32%, and 42%, respectively. This reduction in travel time is significant considering that this improvement results purely from software manipulations and without requiring any new infrastructure.

recommendations.name: Harshada Kunjir

I would highly recommend her design expertise to anyone who needs detailed aesthetic touch in their branding!
